message.content: {"schema_version":"stella.literature_hvs_candidates.v0.1","generated_at":"2026-06-13T03:54:00","paper":{"arxiv_id":"2003.12766","bibcode":"2020A&A...637A..53K","title":"Hypervelocity Stars in the Gaia era. Revisiting the most extreme stars from the MMT survey","month":"2020-03","source_note_json":"notes/2020/2020-03/2020-03.json","links":{"abs":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.12766","pdf":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2003.12766"}},"inputs":{"paper_dir":"literature/2003.12766","audit_path":"literature/2003.12766/audit.json","catalog_review_path":"literature/2003.12766/catalog_review.json","catalog_extraction_path":"literature/2003.12766/catalog_extraction.json","ecsv_paths":["literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-atmos-results.ecsv","literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-B1085.ecsv","literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-hvs5.ecsv","literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-stellar_params.ecsv","literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-kinematic-AS.ecsv","literature/2003.12766/catalog_tables/table-tab-kinematic-TF.ecsv"]},"extraction":{"status":"candidates_found","extracted_at":"2026-06-13T03:54:00","extractor":"agent","summary":"The paper reanalyzes 40 stars from the MMT HVS survey using new spectroscopic models and Gaia DR2 astrometry. 37 stars have full 6D kinematics and are presented as hypervelocity star candidates with bound/unbound probabilities. The extraction includes all 37 kinematic sample stars as candidates possibly unbound from the Milky Way.","tooling":{"agent_runtime":"","model_id":"","prompt_version":"","request_parameters":{}}},"method_chain":[{"id":"step-01","depends_on":[],"step_type":"input_catalog","summary":"MMT HVS survey sample of 42 B-type stars from Brown et al. (2014), restricted to the 40 stars for which MMT spectra are available. Raw spectra are provided for analysis.","inputs":[],"outputs":["MMT raw spectra","object list (40 stars)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":99,"end_line":99,"context":"we extend the spectrophotometric analysis to 40 out of the 42 high-velocity outliers of the MMT HVS sample."}]},{"id":"step-02","depends_on":[],"step_type":"input_catalog","summary":"Multi-band photometry compiled from GALEX, Gaia DR2, SDSS DR12, Pan-STARRS1, SkyMapper DR1.1, BATC, VST-ATLAS-DR3, VST-KiDS-DR3, APASS-DR9, UKIDSS-DR9, and VISTA via VizieR.","inputs":[],"outputs":["multi-band photometry"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":272,"end_line":272,"context":"Photometric data were compiled from a variety of surveys covering the ultraviolet, the optical, and the infrared mainly using the VizieR catalog access tool."}]},{"id":"step-03","depends_on":[],"step_type":"astrometric_calibration","summary":"Proper motions from Gaia DR2 and HST/WFC3+ACS. The more precise measurement is adopted per star: HST for HVS1, HVS10, HVS12, HVS13; Gaia DR2 for B711; all others use Gaia DR2. RUWE < 1.4 verified for all.","inputs":["Gaia DR2 proper motions","HST proper motions"],"outputs":["adopted proper motions (37 stars)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":288,"end_line":288,"context":"Proper motions were mainly taken from Gaia DR2. ... we chose the more precise measurement when Gaia as well as HST proper motions were available, which implies that we used HST data for HVS1, HVS10, HVS12, and HVS13 and Gaia DR2 data for B711."}]},{"id":"step-04","depends_on":["step-01","step-03"],"step_type":"sample_selection","summary":"From the 40 stars with spectra, 37 have complete astrometric data. HVS11, HVS14, HVS23 lack proper motions and are excluded. This step defines the kinematic sample of 37 stars.","inputs":["spectroscopic sample","proper motions"],"outputs":["kinematic sample (37 stars)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":284,"end_line":284,"context":"proper motion measurements were not available for HVS11, HVS14, and HVS23, which is why the kinematic analysis encompasses only 37 out of the 40 stars in the sample."}]},{"id":"step-05","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"radial_velocity_measurement","summary":"Radial velocities obtained from χ² minimization fitting of flux-calibrated MMT spectra against synthetic model spectra. The fit simultaneously solves for radial velocity; other parameters are fitted but their adoption belongs to step-06.","inputs":["MMT flux-calibrated spectra"],"outputs":["radial_velocity"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":150,"end_line":150,"context":"four fitting parameters for the stellar spectrum: the effective temperature T_eff, the surface gravity log(g), the projected rotational velocity v sin i, and the radial velocity v_rad."}]},{"id":"step-06","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"stellar_parameter_inference","summary":"T_eff, log g, and v sin i derived from the same spectral fitting procedure as radial velocities. The ADS grid of synthetic spectra (solar composition, 9000-16000 K, log g 3.0-4.8) is used. Results in Table tab:atmos-results.","inputs":["MMT flux-calibrated spectra","model grid"],"outputs":["T_eff","log_g","v_sin_i"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":150,"end_line":150,"context":"four fitting parameters ... T_eff, log(g), v sin i, and v_rad."},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":110,"end_line":128,"context":"Table tab:atmos-results listing spectroscopic results."}]},{"id":"step-07","depends_on":["step-16","step-08"],"step_type":"distance_estimation","summary":"Spectrophotometric distances computed as d = 2R/Θ, where Θ is the angular diameter from SED fitting (step-16) and R is the stellar radius from evolutionary models (step-08).","inputs":["angular_diameter","stellar_radius"],"outputs":["distance"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":280,"end_line":280,"context":"The resulting angular diameters Θ = 2 R / d were combined with the stellar radii R ... to calculate the spectrophotometric distances d."}]},{"id":"step-08","depends_on":["step-06"],"step_type":"stellar_parameter_inference","summary":"Derivation of stellar masses, ages, radii, luminosities, and Ω/Ω_crit from position in Kiel diagram compared to rotating MS evolutionary tracks (Georgy et al. 2013). Stellar radii feed into distance estimation.","inputs":["T_eff","log_g","evolutionary tracks"],"outputs":["mass","age","radius","luminosity","Omega_over_Omega_crit"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":226,"end_line":226,"context":"stellar masses M, ages τ, radii R, luminosities L, and ratios ... Ω/Ω_crit can be derived by comparing the stars' position in the Kiel diagram with theoretical predictions."}]},{"id":"step-09","depends_on":[],"step_type":"solar_position_and_motion","summary":"Solar position and velocity assumptions for Galactocentric coordinate transformation. Paper states R0 = 8.3 kpc, Sun on negative x-axis. Other parameters (z0, v_circ, solar peculiar motion) are adopted from Irrgang et al. (2013) / Paper II but not explicitly reported here.","inputs":[],"outputs":["solar Galactocentric position and velocity"],"parameters":[{"name":"R0","raw_value":"8.3","value":"8.3","unit":"kpc","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":325,"end_line":325,"context":"the solar radius (dashed; 8.3 kpc)"}]},{"name":"z0","raw_value":"not reported","value":"","description":"Not explicitly stated in this paper; adopted from Irrgang et al. (2013) via Paper II.","source_refs":[]},{"name":"v_circ_sun","raw_value":"not reported","value":"","description":"Not explicitly stated in this paper.","source_refs":[]},{"name":"solar_motion_u","raw_value":"not reported","value":"","description":"Not explicitly stated in this paper.","source_refs":[]},{"name":"solar_motion_v","raw_value":"not reported","value":"","description":"Not explicitly stated in this paper.","source_refs":[]},{"name":"solar_motion_w","raw_value":"not reported","value":"","description":"Not explicitly stated in this paper.","source_refs":[]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":307,"end_line":307,"context":"right-handed Cartesian Galactic coordinate system in which the Sun is located on the negative x-axis and the z-axis points to the Galactic north pole."},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":284,"end_line":284,"context":"The strategy of the kinematic analysis is identical to that of Paper II."}]},{"id":"step-10","depends_on":["step-03","step-05","step-07","step-09"],"step_type":"velocity_calculation","summary":"Construction of full 6D phase space: positions, proper motions, radial velocities, and distances combined to compute Galactocentric Cartesian positions (x,y,z) and velocities (vx,vy,vz). Galactic rest-frame velocity v_Grf = (vx²+vy²+vz²)^(1/2).","inputs":["RA,Dec","proper motions","radial velocities","distances","solar parameters"],"outputs":["galactocentric_x","galactocentric_y","galactocentric_z","galactocentric_vx","galactocentric_vy","galactocentric_vz","v_Grf"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":284,"end_line":284,"context":"Complementing our revised radial velocities and spectrophotometric distances with positions and proper motions ... gave us the full six-dimensional phase space information."}]},{"id":"step-11","depends_on":[],"step_type":"galactic_potential_model","summary":"Two Galactic mass models: Model I (revision of Allen & Santillan 1991) and Model II (Wilkinson & Evans 1999 flat rotation curve). Both calibrated with Gaia DR2 constraints. Used to compute local escape velocities and integrate orbits.","inputs":[],"outputs":["potential_models","escape_velocity"],"parameters":[{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Model I (Allen & Santillan revision)","value":"Model I (Allen & Santillan revision)","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":293,"end_line":293,"context":"Model~I is a revision of the popular model by Allen & Santillan (1991)"}]},{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Model II (Wilkinson & Evans flat rotation)","value":"Model II (Wilkinson & Evans flat rotation)","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":293,"end_line":293,"context":"Model~II is based on the flat rotation curve model by Wilkinson & Evans (1999)"}]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":291,"end_line":293,"context":"The trajectories of all targets were computed in two different Milky Way mass models."}]},{"id":"step-12","depends_on":["step-10","step-11","step-08"],"step_type":"orbit_integration","summary":"Monte Carlo orbit integration with 1.5 million realizations, backwards in time up to 15 Gyr. Correlated proper motion errors from Gaia DR2 included. Plane-crossing events within 99% confidence limit on stellar age are recorded. Flight times and disk-crossing parameters derived.","inputs":["6D phase space","potential models","stellar ages"],"outputs":["orbit_ensembles","plane_crossing_positions","plane_crossing_velocities","flight_time"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":284,"end_line":284,"context":"Propagation of uncertainties ... standard Monte Carlo approach with 1.5 million realizations."}]},{"id":"step-13","depends_on":["step-12","step-11"],"step_type":"escape_or_bound_assessment","summary":"Bound/unbound assessment: local escape velocity v_esc from potential model, compared to v_Grf. Bound probability P_b (%) from fraction of bound Monte Carlo realizations. Values tabulated for both Model I and Model II.","inputs":["v_Grf","potential model"],"outputs":["v_esc","v_Grf_minus_v_esc","P_b"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":307,"end_line":307,"context":"The Galactic rest-frame velocity v_Grf, the local Galactic escape velocity v_esc ... are listed."}]},{"id":"step-14","depends_on":["step-12","step-08"],"step_type":"origin_assessment","summary":"Classification of stars into origin categories: unconstrained, outer rim, disk origin. Ejection velocities v_ej,p computed at plane crossing, correcting for Galactic rotation. Flight times compared to stellar ages.","inputs":["plane_crossing_locations","stellar ages","plane_crossing_velocities"],"outputs":["origin_category","ejection_velocity","origin_site"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":329,"end_line":331,"context":"we group the stars in three categories based on their inferred spatial origin."},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":302,"end_line":302,"context":"the ejection velocity v_ej,p ... is a more useful quantity."}]},{"id":"step-15","depends_on":["step-03"],"step_type":"quality_filter","summary":"Astrometric quality assessment using Gaia DR2 RUWE: all stars have RUWE < 1.4, confirming well-behaved astrometric solutions.","inputs":["Gaia DR2 proper motions"],"outputs":["RUWE"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":288,"end_line":288,"context":"For all objects in the sample, RUWE is below the recommended value of 1.4."}]},{"id":"step-16","depends_on":["step-02","step-06"],"step_type":"photometric_or_sed_modeling","summary":"SED fitting of multi-band photometry with synthetic SEDs (Atlas12 models fixed to spectroscopic T_eff and log g). Angular diameter Θ (scaling factor) and color excess E(B-V) derived via χ² minimization. The angular diameter is used in distance estimation.","inputs":["multi-band photometry","T_eff","log_g"],"outputs":["angular_diameter","E_BminusV"],"parameters":[{"name":"other","raw_value":"R_V = 3.1","value":"3.1","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":276,"end_line":276,"context":"R_V fixed at 3.1, its typical value for the interstellar medium."}]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":276,"end_line":276,"context":"The resulting observed SEDs were compared to synthetic ones ... Only the angular diameter and the color excess were fitted."}]},{"id":"step-17","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"quality_filter","summary":"Spectroscopic data quality assessment from MMT spectral fitting: wavelength-averaged signal-to-noise ratio (S/N_tot), reduced χ² at best fit, and number of individual exposures per star. These quality flags are listed in Table tab:atmos-results.","inputs":["MMT spectra"],"outputs":["S_N_tot","reduced_chi2","N_exposures"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":144,"end_line":144,"context":"the average S/N of the co-added spectra is only of the order of 10--30."},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":110,"end_line":128,"context":"Table tab:atmos-results listing S/N_tot, χ²_red, and of 42 B-type stars with Galactic rest-frame radial velocity exceeding +275 km/s, selected from Brown et al. (2014).","decision":"partially_included","reason":"Of the 42 stars, 40 have MMT spectra analyzed in this paper; 37 have proper motions enabling full kinematic analysis and bound/unbound assessment. Two stars lack spectra; three (HVS11, HVS14, HVS23) lack proper motions.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":99,"end_line":99,"context":"we extend the spectrophotometric analysis to 40 out of the 42 high-velocity outliers of the MMT HVS sample."}]},{"group_id":"kinematic_sample_37","description":"Stars with full 6D kinematics (positions, proper motions, radial velocities, spectrophotometric distances) for which bound/unbound probabilities, escape velocities, and origin assessments are presented.","decision":"included","reason":"The paper presents these 37 stars as HVS candidates with complete kinematic analysis, providing bound probabilities v_Grf - v_esc, and origin categories. They are the primary candidate set.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":284,"end_line":284,"context":"the kinematic analysis encompasses only 37 out of the 40 stars in the sample."},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":307,"end_line":307,"context":"The detailed results of the kinematic analyses are listed in Tables tab:kinematic-AS and tab:kinematic-TF."}]},{"group_id":"alternative_scenarios","description":"Supplementary kinematic rows in Model I/II tables for HVS5 using Brown (2015) parameters, B576 under the BHB assumption (M=0.5 M_sun), and B598 under the ELM WD assumption (M=0.25 M_sun). These are alternative distance/mass scenarios for the same stars.","decision":"excluded","reason":"These rows are not independent candidates but different model assumptions for the same objects. The primary MS-based rows are used for the candidate list. The alternatives are discussed in the paper text for comparison.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":396,"end_line":397,"context":"HVS5: we illustrate the impact of the different distance estimates on the outcome of the kinematic analysis."},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":420,"end_line":429,"context":"B576: Assuming a MS nature ... it would be the only star in the sample for which an origin in the Galactic disk could be ruled out ... However, the measured rotational velocity ... make it much more plausible that B576 is actually a low-mass BHB star."},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":398,"end_line":418,"context":"B598: a puzzling object ... if B598 was a MS star, it would be one of our best candidates for the ejection by the Hills mechanism ... However, the star is likely an evolved low mass star of ~0.2 M_sun."}]},{"group_id":"missing_spectra","description":"Two stars from the original 42-star MMT sample for which MMT spectra were not available to the authors.","decision":"excluded","reason":"Without spectra, spectrophotometric analysis is impossible; these objects cannot be assessed as HVS candidates in this work.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":99,"end_line":99,"context":"We did not have access to the spectra of the two missing object."}]},{"group_id":"missing_proper_motions","description":"Stars HVS11, HVS14, and HVS23 which have MMT spectra and derived atmospheric/stellar parameters but lack proper motion measurements from Gaia DR2 or HST.","decision":"excluded","reason":"Without proper motions, the 6D phase space is incomplete; Galactic rest-frame velocities cannot be computed and unbound status cannot be assessed.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2003.12766/arxiv_source/aa.tex","start_line":284,"end_line":284,"context":"proper motion measurements were not available for HVS11, HVS14, and HVS23, which is why the kinematic analysis encompasses only 37 out of the 40 stars in the sample."}]}]}
message.reasoning_content:
We need to fix the method_chain: step-07 and step-15 are used as direct producers for incompatible quantity categories. The error says: "method step 'step-07' is used as direct producer for incompatible quantity categories ['distance', 'photometric']; split this method step". Similarly for step-15: ['quality', 'stellar_parameter'].

The method_chain currently has step-07 as "distance_estimation" producing distance, angular diameter, E(B-V). But also, photometric quantities? In the candidate stage (stage 2 perhaps) we haven't yet filled candidate details. However, the error indicates that the method step's step_type is assigned to multiple categories that are incompatible as per the validator. The step_type must correspond to a canonical step_type, and the validator likely checks that a method step of type "distance_estimation" should not be used as direct producer for photometric fields. But step-07 also produces E(B-V) which is a photometric quantity (extinction). Actually the step summary includes "distance", "angular_diameter", "E_BminusV". The step_type "distance_estimation" is appropriate for distance, but extinction (E(B-V)) is photometric, and that might need a separate step. The paper's SED fitting derives both angular diameter and reddening simultaneously, but to satisfy the schema, we need to split that into a separate step for photometric/reddening, and keep distance estimation. Or we can adjust the step_type to not be incorrectly assigned. The error says the step is used as direct producer for incompatible quantity categories ['distance', 'photometric']. So the validator sees that step-07 is referenced in candidate quantities for both distance (core.observed_phase_space.distance) and photometry (photometry[] with measurement_type 'extinction' or 'reddening'). That is not allowed. We need to split step-07 into two steps: one for distance estimation (step-07) and another for photometric reddening/angular diameter fitting (new step, e.g., step-16). Then candidates will reference the appropriate step for the respective quantities.

Similarly, step-15 is "quality_filter" that produced quality flags (e.g., S/N, RUWE) but also some stellar parameters? Let's check step-15 summary: "Quality assessment of MMT spectral data: wavelength-averaged S/N, reduced χ² at best fit, number of individual exposures per star. These metrics appear in Table tab:atmos-results as quality indicators. RUWE from Gaia DR2 used for astrometric quality (all <1.4)." Outputs: "S_N", "reduced_chi2", "N_exposures", "RUWE". S/N and reduced chi-squared and N exposures are quality flags, but technically stellar parameter? However, the validator says it's used for quality and stellar_parameter categories. Maybe we assigned it as direct producer for some stellar parameter? In the scaffold, we haven't filled candidate details, but the validator might predict that the step's outputs include stellar_parameter fields like "S/N" maybe. Or maybe the step_type "quality_filter" can only produce quality flags; we cannot have it produce stellar parameters. In our scaffold, we listed S/N etc. as outputs, but in candidate fields they would be placed under quality_flags[] not under stellar_parameters. The issue may be that the validator sees step-15's step_type "quality_filter" is used as direct producer for a stellar_parameter quantity. That could be something like "spectral S/N" might be confusing. To fix, we need to separate the spectroscopic quality metrics (S/N, chi2) from the quality filter. But maybe the validator is stricter: step_type "quality_filter" cannot produce anything other than quality flags. So if we later plan to put S/N as a quality flag, that's fine, but the step also produces something like "stellar_parameter"? Not yet. The error could be anticipating that the step's output includes "S/N" which might be mistakenly classified as a stellar parameter. To be safe, we should split step-15 into a step that does spectroscopic quality assessment (step-15a) and a separate step for RUWE astrometric quality? Actually the error says it's used for incompatible categories ['quality', 'stellar_parameter']. So likely some candidate quantity of type "stellar_parameters" references step-15. In the scaffold we haven't yet referenced step-15 in any candidate quantity because we only have identifiers. But the validator might be checking the method_chain itself: it sees that step-15 is type "quality_filter" but its outputs include "S_N" and "reduced_chi2". The machine might think those are stellar_parameters because they are derived from spectra? But the step_type is "quality_filter", so maybe it's okay. The error message says "method step 'step-15' is used as direct producer for incompatible quantity categories ['quality', 'stellar_parameter']". That suggests that in the candidate records (which are not yet filled) the reference to step-15 would appear in both quality_flags and stellar_parameters. But we haven't filled those. Possibly the validator is forward-looking based on the method_chain outputs and step_type? Hmm. Actually, the error might come from the method_chain's own internal consistency: the validator may check that the outputs of a step, if placed in different typed groups in candidates, must correspond to a step_type that can produce both. The rule is that a method step cannot be a direct producer for multiple mutually exclusive quantity categories. For step-15, we listed outputs: S/N, reduced chi2, N_exposures, RUWE. In the candidate schema, S/N would go under spectroscopy (measurement_type signal_to_noise), not exactly quality_flag, but spectroscopy.
